item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lmao reminds me of Youtube "Prank" channels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Imagery is on point. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're thinking of SCP-294.

item.title: Laugh is indeed fun!
item.content:encoded: This is genuinely funny, in a Dadaist, "what the [EXPLETIVE]" way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Woah. This is one of the first skips I read, and going back to it now it's still as unsettling. It isn't a spooky monster, and it isn't a Thing That Will Kill You. (maybe). Earlier someone said that it was a skip that broke their mainstream view on SCP's, and I totally agree with that. (Although for me the first "non-monster" skip was the coffee machine I can't remember the number of.) This is devolving into almost as much nonsense as Mr.Laugh guy himself. Whatever.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Crosslinking is a pretty normal thing, it allow you to link to related subjects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the article I noticed links leading to other SCPs on random words. Not sure if this is supposed to mean something (besides the acronym one); even so, shouldn’t they be in footnotes or parentheses?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Could it be…. Satan?

Seriously though, it could be Satan. Or Lucifer. Or whatever. That shit is terrifying.

(+1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So there's a Quebec-based show here in Canada called Just For Laughs: Gags that has this unsettling vibe to it that makes it feel like any second it'll devolve into this scip and it wouldn't feel at all out of place.

Anyway, for that reason and many others, this is by far one of the scips I think about the most. That imagery, man. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Terrifyingly good in all it's disturbing glory.
It's just a prank, bro.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is absolutely terrifying for reasons I can't really put my finger on. Maybe it's the implication that these missing people are in some sort of America's Funniest Home Videos purgatory for the rest of time. Maybe it's that I just find knockoffs creepy.

Anyway, +1. It's really good!! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interpretation: this is what a Sarkic comedy show looks like. Notice all the episodes involve organic manipulation. The people involved are all Sarkicists - the reason they're afraid at the beginning is that they fear they've messed up some experiment/ritual they performed and are about to die, and the reason they calm down and there are no dead bodies found afterwards is because the show is well known in the Sarkic community, as is the fact that the organic manipulations SCP-2030-1 performs are harmless and reversible. An earlier version of this article had the last speech end with "We hunger". What is the endonym for Sarkicism again? Ah, yes, "Nälkä", meaning "hunger".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noting that Revision 47 was made without authorial permission, and has been reverted to revision 46.

item.title: Re: HELPMEH
item.content:encoded: I believe the suggestion Krueger was making was that "Laugh is Fun" was an actual hidden-camera prank show at some point in its history, and a competitor for America's Funniest Home Videos. However, when AFV got a new host, Tom Bergeron, it became far more successful, and Laugh is Fun had to come up with something to regain its ratings. The producers (or whoever) made a deal-with-the-devil type of thing, figuring if they couldn't get as good of a host, they could increase how drastic their 'pranks' were. The entity they contacted thrived on laughter, so this worked for it, and they wanted something more drastic, so this worked for them. Unfortunately for both, the entity they contacted didn't quite understand what people find funny, or that this level of anomaly would get them contained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the fourth part is gonna give me nightmares =3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some strange reason I imagine SCP-2030-1 as sounding a whole lot like Cave Johnson J. K. Simmons…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One interesting thing about the tie-in with cousin johnny is that scp-3004 implies that cousin johnny thinks he's the jewish biblical god and is still terrified of this thing. That combined with the fact that scp-2030-1 is apparently very good at making people trust it wears a generic suit and does…the crazy shit it does means that scp-2030-1 might be the devil. Not lucifer-ish or a possibly satanic object like scp-035 but legitimately satan. And honestly it's probably one of the only monsters in horror fiction that could live up to it.

item.title: I don't know what I just read...
item.content:encoded: But it was absolutely amazing. Instant +1. Very well done - the kind of insanity that you don't realize is there until the very end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Crosslinked Funerals Are Fun because it's incredible and everyone should read it.

item.title: I blame you
item.content:encoded: I blame you for everyone calling me 2030… Anyways good writing about me and thanks for not revealing my face! Anyways see you in a month for the Easter episode! And remember LAUGHISFUN!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Crosslinked with SCP-2535.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Years later and we're still finding mistakes. Truly the gift that keeps on giving!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He refers to himself as "Laughy McLaugherson".

Minor quibble, should be it and itself :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For a first SCP in over 3 months, this is amazing. The examples of "Pranks" on the show are really fun to read, and the concept really great. The idea of an anomalous hidden camera prank show is really cool, and the execution is really well done. I could really get an idea of the behaviors of SCP-2030-1 just from the image of him. +1 from me for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I definitely like this one, but I think it might be a bit more creepy if it laid off on the "it magically appears online" angle and just played it as a physically mundane but absolutely real record of anomalous events.

It's supernatural enough that it's easy to discount the entire thing as unreal, when it would be a lot scarier to drive home the idea that this is a show that is actually being produced by somebody, like an inverse Candle Cove.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. Read it before I got my membership, and I still love it today.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That video though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of my all-time favorites! What a beautifully written SCP. Really shows the insanely cool things your mind can come up with when you let it run wild. I'd +1 this twice if I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Headcanon : In the "kid version" claimed in the article, 2030-1 dresses as a familiar clown we all know and love, *cough*993*cough*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reading this article made me feel like I was in a fever dream. Delightfully trippy, with the right amount of creepy, I thoroughly enjoyed the sensation of hair pricking on the back of my neck. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the general creepiness of this, and how it connects/relates with a part of people's everyday life: television. This allows people to have their feeling of security cracked, as they will likely watch tv and think of this. I would like to see an addendum or tale where a body is found after a recent attack, or something to better understand what exactly the spokesman is and what happens to the bodies. Like, does what happens on the show happen to the victims? For instance, the man who got 8 heads. Would his corpse be found with these 8 heads as the cause of death? Other than that, I like this piece, and it definitely gets my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this one, but something about the actual episodes just fell a little flat for me. Just, extremely strange and unclear if the people in the episodes are the actual people who died or not. If they were abducted or what. Anyways, still a creepy show for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rick Sanchez probably watches this show and genuinely gets a kick out of it.

As Kaktus said (waaaaaaaaaaay) earlier, this is indeed one of those scips where I laughed, but felt like I probably shouldn't have. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted, and yeah, I'm Totally Going To Hell For This.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote. Yeah it's weird and kind of creepy but it just seems a little too….. disconnected? People disappear and horribly creepy stuff happens to them on TV and then the host walks out and they laugh. It just seems out of focus a bit, the creepy laugh bit at the end almost seemed like it was just tacked on so that it would have a purpose. Still, not bad.

item.title: Re: HELPMEH
item.content:encoded: Nothing, forget it.

item.title: Re: HELPMEH
item.content:encoded: The high holy hell were you trying to say?

item.title: HELPMEH
item.content:encoded:
2 things.
1.I have a problem with this and not because of the writing or anything, in fact the writing is pretty good.It's pretty much a personal problem for me, and that is when ever the host says LAUGH with us, I constantly hear either THIS:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=a9ANc2rGWoE
or THIS:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UnmYfhXPl7w
Please help me,SOMEBODY!
2.The reason that the show is like this is because of Tom Bergeron, need I say more? Yes? Tom made AFV so much more popular,LAUGH IS FUN had to go to way more extreme measures to get back ratings.A bit too extreme actually.

item.title: Pretty good SCP I think.
item.content:encoded:
I love this SCP because it seems you could encounter this "show" any day on Netflix and Hulu or any other form of digital distribution and see this disturbing show. It's a lot like SCP-993 because it seems like a innocent tv show but it isn't. I also like the anonymous aspect of Laughy Mclaugherson because he's the one behind all of this but you don't quite know who he is.

1+ from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reading this has officially inspired me to try and write my own SCP.

Although, the Margret Thatcher incident is going to haunt me for the rest of my days.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Just how DID she get there?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great! One of the better SCPs I have read in Series 3.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is like "It's just a prank bro!!" the SCP. Well done, reminds me of that evil clown and also the one with all the perverse videos that mess your mind up when you watch them. Unsettling and well executed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
come to think of it,
this came before (most) of those overly-fucked up pranks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Two brothers… On a game show…"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is like a show you would see on Rick and Morty

item.title: bro it just a prank
item.content:encoded: BRO BRO IT'S JUST A LAUGH BRO IT'S JUST A LAUGH SOCIAL LAUGHPERIMENT!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
HOLY GOOD [EXPLETIVE DELETED] S-[EXPLETIVE DELETED] WAN[ENOUGH WITH THE SWEARING] THIS SCP IS A FUCKED UP VERSION OF JUST FOR LAUGHS:GAGS

+1 THAT WAS FUCKED UP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So where's the tape where SCP-2030-1 gets tased by police officers after shouting "ITS JUST A PRANK BRO! ITS JUST A PRANK" a couple of times?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh fuck, it's Candid Camera meets Laugh-In from Hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hate this. I hate this article with a passion. Everything in this article is wrong, and everything makes me want to throw sharp objects at you. Or disembowel you. Or have you star in this show.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 1993 is when it was first discovered, but that doesn't mean it didn't exist before then- only that manifestations before that date weren't recorded, or the records haven't been found.

item.title: IMO This show takes place in hell.
item.content:encoded: I mean look at it, it stars dead people, they can endure severe bodily harm and not die, basically hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well that was fucked up. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just want to give the author a big Thank You for introducing me to the wonderful world of SCPs. Prior to finding this I just assumed all SCPs were just humanoid/reptilian creatures. Bored one day, I clicked on a Top 10 SCP vid on youtube. This eerily brilliant masterpiece shattered all my preconceptions.

Questions though: when the themes of episodes are listed (candy, pets, etc) , are those the actual episode names? If so, shouldnt they be capitalized in quotes? If you are just listing general themes, shouldnt the themes appear without apostrophes? Sorry not too familiar with site conventions.

Also regarding when it says "filmed before a studio audience" (notice it didnt say 'live' studio audience lol) The document notes that the audience is comprised of segment victims. Wouldnt that limit the audience to just 2-5 people given the nature and length of the episodes? Im just curious. Every little detail of this piece fascinates me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Its Keter because its largely uncontrollable and can pop up pretty much anywhere, its very poorly understood in terms of its methodology, and seeming kills all its victims.

These arent just ordinary kidnappings (if they even are that—nobody knows). Did you read the episode logs? Crazy stuff happens and victims somehow "know" the host? Too many anomalous and unknown factors here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is this a Keter SCP? I don't really understand the Supernatural qualities or what makes this so dangerous. If anything, it just seems like common kidnappings and/or an actual game show.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If someone does decide to edit this, please add more so as to explain why this is a Keter class SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would +1 for the Martin Freeman octopus alone.

Laughs/cries simultaneously

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gah!

Gah!
Candid Camera by way of Korrok!
+1
Gah!
I really have no idea why this one messed with my head so much, but it did.
